message:
fix: harden allocator locality, WAL replay integrity, and ext4 fsck safety

ffs-alloc: Add locality-aware inode allocation that tries nearby groups
(within 8 of the target) before falling back to a full linear scan. This
reduces inode fragmentation for directory-heavy workloads. Also adds 7
proptest property-based tests for bitmap edge cases (OOB start, zero
count, overscan, contiguous request exceeding count, empty bitmap).

ffs-core: Cap btrfs symlink read size to PATH_MAX (4096) instead of
blindly casting attr.size to u32 (which could yield u32::MAX). In the
ext4 integrity verifier, replace three usize::try_from().unwrap_or(usize::MAX)
patterns with proper error handling that emits CheckVerdict failures
instead of silently using a sentinel value that would cause out-of-bounds
accesses on 32-bit targets.

ffs-mvcc/wal: Detect zero-record-length with non-zero tail as corruption
rather than silently treating it as end-of-data. Add trailing payload
validation before CRC to catch structural corruption. Use checked_add
in commit_byte_size to prevent overflow.

ffs-mvcc/persist: Add WAL tail truncation after replay to remove
discarded/corrupted bytes, preventing re-parsing on subsequent opens.
Enforce strictly monotonic commit sequence during replay and reject
duplicate or descending sequences. Reserve u64::MAX for sentinel use.
Includes regression tests for truncated WAL recovery, duplicate commit
detection, and trailing payload rejection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
